title: DLRS .life Asset Genesis
description: Provenance record for a single asset inside a `.life` package. v0.8 (asset architecture epic, sub-issue #101). Every asset declared in `manifest/manifest.json` SHOULD have a sibling `genesis/<asset_id>.genesis.json` that answers four questions: what method produced it, what inputs fed in, where compute happened, and which consent scopes authorised the production. The genesis file is what makes withdrawal cascades, hosted-API leak audits, license disputes, and reproducibility checks tractable. See docs/LIFE_GENESIS_SPEC.md for the normative text.

schema_version.description: Version of this schema. Bumped on any breaking change. Loaders MUST refuse genesis files with an unknown schema_version.

asset_id.description: Identifier of the asset this genesis describes. MUST equal the `asset_id` declared for the corresponding entry in `manifest/manifest.json`.

asset_kind.description: Optional asset-kind tag. Mirrors the manifest entry; copied here so genesis is self-describing without forcing the loader to also load the manifest. Free-form string is allowed because asset kinds evolve faster than this schema.

method.description: Identifies the build process that produced the asset. Decision D3=C: methods are not required to be bit-identical reproducible, but they MUST be identifiable.

name.description: Canonical method identifier. Examples: `xtts-v2-finetune`, `paragraph-memory-atoms`, `regex-knowledge-graph`. Free-form, but stable: the same method MUST always use the same name.

version.description: Method version. SemVer or commit-SHA fragment. Used together with code_commit for reproducibility checks.

config_ref.description: Optional path (inside `.life`) to a JSON/YAML file containing the full training/build configuration (hyperparameters, seed, dataset filters). Loaders MAY hash this file to detect drift across re-runs.

code_repo.description: Optional URL of the source repository implementing the method. Helpful but not required (closed-source methods may omit it).

code_commit.description: Optional commit SHA pinning the implementation. Required when reproducibility_level is `bit_identical` or `param_identical`.

source_inputs.description: Every input artefact consumed during production, in declared order. The combination of source_inputs + method.code_commit + method.config_ref is what makes a build reproducible (when reproducibility_level allows). Empty arrays are forbidden: a fully-synthetic asset that takes no inputs SHOULD declare a single virtual input pointing at the base model (decision D1=C).

type.description: What kind of input this is. `recording` = original media file. `text` = text artefact. `derived` = an upstream pipeline output (e.g. cleaned transcript). `base_model` = a pretrained model used as a starting point — declared as a virtual input per decision D1=C so the package is self-describing. `dataset` = a public/curated corpus.

ref.description: Path inside the `.life` zip OR an external URI. Internal paths are required for `recording`, `text`, `image`, `video`, `derived`. External URIs are allowed for `base_model` and `dataset` (where the artefact lives on a public registry).

sha256.description: SHA-256 of the input bytes, hex. Case-insensitive. For `base_model` virtual inputs the hash MUST be of the published weights file; loaders MAY skip the hash check on such inputs but MUST surface a warning when they do.

consent_ref.description: Pointer to the consent evidence covering this input. Either a path inside `consent/` (e.g. `consent/recording-2025-01-12.signed.pdf`) or `not_applicable` for inputs that do not carry per-input consent (a public base model under permissive license, a public dataset).

consent_scope.description: Subset of the package-level `consent_scope` enum (decision D4=A) that THIS input was authorised for. Empty arrays are rejected. For `base_model` / `dataset` inputs whose use is governed by license rather than consent, declare the single value `license_governed`.

license.description: Optional license tag. REQUIRED for `base_model` and `dataset` inputs (to support license-compatibility audits). Examples: `apache-2.0`, `mit`, `cc-by-nc-4.0`, `proprietary-eula`.

source_url.description: Optional canonical URL (publication, model card, registry page). RECOMMENDED for `base_model` and `dataset` inputs.

compute.description: Where and when production happened. Decision D2=B: hosted-API use is declared and surfaced, but does NOT block ingestion. Loaders weigh hosted-API exposure via the tier system (#104) instead.

platform.description: Free-form description of the compute environment. Examples: `local-mac-m2`, `local-linux-rtx4090`, `aws-ec2-g5.xlarge`, `colab-pro-t4`. Used for forensic auditing, not policy.

operator.description: Identifier (email, DID, registry handle) of the natural or legal person who actually ran the build. MUST match an `issued_by.identifier` of the package OR an authorised `operator` declared in policy. Loaders MAY reject mismatches.

started_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the build started.

finished_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the build finished writing the asset. MUST be greater than or equal to started_at.

hosted_api_used.description: True iff any non-local API saw input bytes during production. The `policy/hosted_api.json` opt-in gate (v0.6) controls runtime hosted-API calls; THIS field is independent and concerns build time.

hosted_api_providers.description: REQUIRED when hosted_api_used is true. Each entry names a provider that saw input bytes. Used by leak-detection audits and by tier.audit_chain_strength scoring.

data_left_local.description: True iff the build kept all source bytes on the operator's local infrastructure (hosted_api_used MAY still be true if only derived outputs were sent off-machine). Used by privacy-conscious loaders to filter packages.

gpu.description: Optional GPU identifier (e.g. `nvidia-rtx-4090`, `apple-m2-max`). Helps with reproducibility (`bit_identical` is GPU-sensitive).

consent_scope_checked.description: Records that the build process actually verified, at production time, that the consent evidence covered the scopes used. This is what makes consent enforcement machine-checkable rather than aspirational.

verified.description: True iff a verifier confirmed every entry in scopes_used was authorised by the consent evidence at verified_at. Loaders MUST refuse genesis files with `verified: false` unless an explicit overrides policy is in effect.

verifier.description: Identifier of the tool that performed verification. Examples: `tools/build_life_package.py`, `dlrs-runtime/0.9.0`.

verifier_version.description: Version of the verifier. SemVer or commit fragment.

scopes_used.description: Every consent scope this build relied on. MUST be a subset of the package-level `consent_scope` and of the union of source_inputs[*].consent_scope.

audit_event_ref.description: Pointer into the package-level audit log. Format: `audit/<filename>#L<line>` matching the existing v0.6 descriptor-audit bridge convention.

audit_event_id.description: Stable id of the audit event recording the asset's birth. ULID/UUID4 or any non-empty machine identifier. The audit event itself MUST exist in audit/events.jsonl with action=`asset_created` and refer back to this asset_id.

reproducibility_level.description: Decision D3=C, graded enum. `bit_identical` = re-running the method with the same source_inputs, code_commit, and config_ref produces byte-identical output (rare; requires deterministic kernels and pinned RNGs). `param_identical` = same training inputs and method but model parameters may differ across runs due to non-deterministic CUDA/GPU kernels. `not_reproducible` = retraining will not yield equivalent assets (e.g. hosted-API outputs the operator cannot replay).

consent_scope.description: Decision D4=A, fixed enum. The set of scopes the asset is authorised for. Loaders MUST refuse to use the asset for purposes outside this set, regardless of what the binding/runtime asks for. New scopes are added by spec versions; older packages stay valid.

base_model.description: Decision D1=C. RECOMMENDED block for fine-tuned assets (voice clones, persona adapters, …). When present, the same base model SHOULD also appear as a `base_model` source input so license tracing works in both directions.

notes.description: Optional free-form notes. NOT a substitute for required fields.

consent_scope_enum.description: v0.8 fixed consent-scope vocabulary. New entries are added by spec versions. The list is intentionally short to force issuers to think about each scope rather than dumping a free-form description.
